caption: Realistic frames can still hallucinate: OATH measures temporal persistence failures through occlusion debt.
raw_prompt: A professional academic figure for a NeurIPS paper. Show a left-to-right conceptual illustration with three panels. Left panel: a short generated video strip with four frames of a cup on a table, where the cup is briefly occluded by a hand and then incorrectly disappears; add subtle red warning marks over the inconsistent final frame. Middle panel: a taxonomy box with three stacked categories labeled 'Perceptual errors', 'Temporal errors', and 'World-state errors', with the 'Temporal errors' and 'World-state errors' categories highlighted in blue. Right panel: an OATH scoring concept where arrows connect pre-occlusion state, occlusion interval, and post-occlusion state into a box labeled 'Occlusion Debt', producing a scalar hallucination score and a small space-time heatmap. Use clean vector-style lines on a white background, blue and gray boxes, orange accents for hallucination regions, and legible labels suitable for top-tier conference publication.
full_prompt:
Create a professional academic figure for the 'Introduction' section of a research paper about: [cv] I want to quantify the hallucination exhibited in the generated results of video models.

Figure description: A professional academic figure for a NeurIPS paper. Show a left-to-right conceptual illustration with three panels. Left panel: a short generated video strip with four frames of a cup on a table, where the cup is briefly occluded by a hand and then incorrectly disappears; add subtle red warning marks over the inconsistent final frame. Middle panel: a taxonomy box with three stacked categories labeled 'Perceptual errors', 'Temporal errors', and 'World-state errors', with the 'Temporal errors' and 'World-state errors' categories highlighted in blue. Right panel: an OATH scoring concept where arrows connect pre-occlusion state, occlusion interval, and post-occlusion state into a box labeled 'Occlusion Debt', producing a scalar hallucination score and a small space-time heatmap. Use clean vector-style lines on a white background, blue and gray boxes, orange accents for hallucination regions, and legible labels suitable for top-tier conference publication.

Style guidelines:
Simple, clean diagram illustrating a key concept or intuition. Include before/after or problem/solution comparison. Keep it understandable at a glance.

 The image should be in a clean, professional ACADEMIC style suitable for a top-tier AI/ML research paper (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR). Use a white or light background. Use clear labels and annotations. Avoid excessive decoration. Use a consistent color palette. Text should be legible at column width (~3.25 inches). Style: technical illustration, vector-like, clean lines.

caption: Overview of OATH: generated video is converted into tracklets and occlusion events, matched across transitions, and aggregated into an occlusion-debt hallucination score.
raw_prompt: A professional academic figure for a NeurIPS paper. Show a left-to-right pipeline with five major stages. Stage 1: input prompt and a strip of generated video frames. Stage 2: tracking and segmentation module producing colored tracklets and visibility labels over time. Stage 3: occlusion-event detector marking visible-to-occluded-to-visible transitions on a timeline. Stage 4: identity-free matching block with a similarity matrix and arrows linking pre- and post-occlusion entities. Stage 5: debt aggregation module outputting a scalar OATH score and a small space-time heatmap. Use blue boxes for main modules, gray arrows for data flow, orange highlights for hallucination regions, and concise labels under each stage. Clean vector style, white background, top-tier conference quality, readable at column width.

caption: Identity-free matching in OATH compares pre- and post-occlusion candidates through spatial, appearance, motion, and semantic similarity before debt is assigned.
raw_prompt: A professional academic figure for a NeurIPS paper. Show a centered flowchart with three columns. Left column: pre-occlusion entities displayed as colored boxes over a frame, labeled e1, e2, e3. Middle column: a similarity matrix combining spatial overlap, appearance embedding cosine similarity, motion consistency, and VLM semantic similarity; annotate the matrix with weighted terms. Right column: post-occlusion candidate entities in the next visible frame, with arrows from left to right indicating the best identity-free assignments. Beneath the arrows, show two outcomes: low debt for coherent continuation and high debt for disappearance or implausible reassignment. Use blue and gray for structure, orange/red for debt, clean vector lines, white background, legible labels, and a polished conference style.
